Designed for HR admins, this feature helps keep Employee Central data clean and reliable, by default, with full administrative control.

This new capability is designed with real‑world HR operations in mind, especially in environments where future changes are planned in advance. The feature is inspired by the Customer Influence idea Customer Influence Idea 249640.

The Challenge

In many organizations, employments have future‑dated Job Information and Compensation Information records, including recurring pay components, already scheduled.

When an employment is terminated, these future‑dated records for the affected employment often remain in the system. As a result:

  • HR administrators must manually identify and delete records
  • Operational effort increases during the already time‑sensitive termination processes
  • Lingering records can lead to data inconsistencies, potentially impacting reporting and payroll

For organizations managing complex or high‑volume terminations, this manual cleanup quickly becomes a recurring administrative burden.

The Solution

The new automated deletion feature addresses this challenge directly.

Once configured, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central automatically deletes future‑dated Job Information and Compensation Information records that fall after the (Job Information) termination date of an employment. The automation applies only to the terminated employment, ensuring that any other active employment for the same person remains unaffected.

The feature works consistently across all termination channels, including:

  • Web UI
  • Mobile
  • APIs
  • Imports

 This ensures reliable behavior regardless of how employment terminations are processed.

How It Works

Administrators can enable the “Delete Future‑Dated Records After Termination” setting for specific event reasons used in their business processes.

New setting in Event Reason Configuration

  • All future‑dated Job Information and Compensation Information records (including recurring pay components) with an event reason for which the setting is enabled are automatically removed
  • The deletion applies only to the terminated employment
  • The system performs the cleanup without any additional manual steps
  • All deletions are captured in audit logs, supporting transparency and compliance requirements

The feature is enabled by default but remains fully under administrative control. System admins can determine which event reasons should result in a deletion.

Example: Terminate an employment on May 1, 2026 

Note: A Termination entered for May 1, 2026 results in a Job Information Termination Date of May 2, 2026 - the first day an employment becomes inactive. 

Data before Termination 

Job Information History with an upcoming promotion effective June 1, 2026

Compensation Information History with an upcoming promotion effective June 1, 2026

 

Data After Termination 

Job Information History after Termination - the future dated promotion was automatically deleted

Compensation Information History after Termination - the future-dated Promotion was automatically deleted

Want to see the feature in action – watch the demo video.

What This Means for You

Less manual cleanup, fewer data inconsistencies, and more time for your HR teams to focus on strategic work. It’s a practical improvement that makes day‑to‑day operations smoother.

Learn More and Take Action

To explore configuration details and include this feature in your implementations or system optimization activities:

  • Review the feature in the What’s New Viewer: Automated Deletion of Future-Dated Job and Compensation Information Records After Employment Termination
  • Visit the original SAP Customer Influence Idea (249640) that inspired this enhancement.
  • Review the implementation guide for this feature: Creating Event Reasons for Employee Central | SAP Help Portal

Join the conversation

Have you started using automated deletion after employment termination, or are you planning to enable it? Share your thoughts, questions, or feedback in the comments below. We’d love to hear from you.

entry.title: Recurring Deduction Integration in SAP EC: Challenges, Limitations & Workaround
entry.content:
Introduction

In one of our recent Employee Central implementations, we encountered a requirement to integrate data from a custom MDF object into the standard Recurring Deduction object. While this initially appeared to be a straightforward integration scenario, further analysis revealed system limitations that required a different approach. This blog outlines the challenge, solution design, and key learnings from this experience.

Business Requirement

The requirement was to: Extract data from a custom MDF object

Push the data into the Recurring Deduction object

Ensure timely processing aligned with payroll needs

Challenges Faced

The initial approach was to use standard Employee Central APIs.

However, during analysis, we identified a key limitation: the Recurring Deduction object does not have API visibility enabled in Object Definition.

We explored whether standard upsert mechanisms could be used as a workaround. However, the issue was not related to payload or mapping—it was due to the object not being exposed via API.

Solution Design

Given this limitation, we redesigned the integration approach instead of forcing a direct API-based solution.

The final design included SAP CPI and SFTP-based processing:

Data was extracted from MDF and sent to CPI

CPI transformed the data and placed it in an SFTP location

The file was then picked and processed into the Recurring Deduction object

Implementation Approach

To support this design, we implemented a two-job mechanism:

Job 1 (CPI): Extract MDF data and post it to the SFTP folder

Job 2 (Provisioning Job): Pick the file from SFTP and post it into the Recurring Deduction object

Additional Challenge

The client required the data to be available immediately (near real-time).

While SAP CPI allows multiple executions per day, the Provisioning job responsible for processing files from SFTP does not support the same level of scheduling flexibility.

Initially, this appeared to be a limitation that could impact the requirement.

Workaround Implemented

To meet the requirement, we implemented the following approach:

Created multiple identical Provisioning jobs (5 jobs)

Scheduled them at different intervals (staggered hourly)

Aligned CPI execution timing to ensure file readiness

Additionally, we maintained a "Modified Since Last Run" filter during data extraction. This ensured that only incremental data was processed and prevented duplicate records.

Outcome

With this approach:

Data was processed multiple times a day

Near real-time expectation was achieved

Duplicate records were avoided

The solution remained stable and aligned with system capabilities

Key Learnings

Not all Employee Central objects are API-enabled

System limitations should guide integration design

Direct approaches are not always feasible or optimal

Practical workarounds can effectively meet business needs

Incremental data handling is critical to avoid duplication

Conclusion

This scenario reinforced an important lesson: successful integration design is not just about technical capability, but about aligning with how the system is designed to operate.

Understanding these boundaries helps in building solutions that are both effective and sustainable.

entry.title: Q1 2026 Innovations: SAP Enterprise Service Management
entry.content:
Register for our Q1 2026 Service Solutions webcast

2026 will prove to be a transformational year for many companies as AI assistants and agents drive high value in service delivery. I’m already excited about the Q2 Innovations blog! Get registered and ready for a preview at Sapphire 2026 of how SAP’s embedded AI innovations are delivering value for customers every day. But first things first, let me highlight some key innovations delivered in Q1 for SAP Enterprise Service Management (SAP ESM).

Ensuring seamless look and feel throughout a unified workspace

Delivering the right user experience has high impact on value and adoption for service specialists especially when the ESM workspace includes third party applications and processes. Using mashups is the preferred method to create an integrated environment ensuring service representatives don’t have to login to different systems or open sperate windows. Now the configuration for mashups has been extended to include theme parameters. With this setting the embedded mashup will adjust automatically to the system set theme providing a consistent look and feel for SAP and 3rd party embedded solutions.

Deliver connected experiences and activity across solutions

The event hub service in BTP allows solutions to publish inbound and outbound events ensuring others can access these activities for visibility and execution of line of business specific processes. SAP ESM now publishes outbound events to the SAP BTP Cloud Application Event Hub so other applications  receive them ensuring a unified and transparent experience across systems. This simplifies the need for system-to-system integration and centralizes events. This transparency can be realized with SAP SuccessFactors ensuring case updates are provided on employee service interactions directly on the SuccessFactors home page.

Self-Service available anytime, anywhere

Employees can get instant answers and create cases from the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile app providing easy 24/7 access for HR support. This provides greater flexibility and ease of access for HR related inquiries and gives employees the ability to work from their channel of choice, even if they are on the go. HR Case management is available as well, allowing creation of new cases, viewing and updating existing cases from wherever they are. Additionally, employees receive notifications when action is needed, ensuring case resolution without response delays.

Accelerating case resolution with UX simplicity

An approval process can delay case resolutions. Working with our customers’ inputs, several improvements have been made that reduce time, ensure consistency and enable a more streamlined handling of cases where approvals are needed. Ensuring quick steps to final resolution, both Service reps and managers benefit from a more efficient process and consistency while approvals are in progress. The following capabilities have been enabled to accelerate the approval process:

  • Approval steps enabled in auto flow scenarios
  • Case status triggers notifications or email
  • Directly approve from case header
  • Easy access to visibility in next steps and status
  • Read-only case state during approval steps


Flexing AI to accelerate case resolution

Another key improvement in Q1 includes extending the business information extraction capability to include custom fields. This capability saves time, reduces errors and delivers ease to service teams – eliminating the need to scour through email for relevant information and then having to make several manual entries. SAP to SAP solutions is rather logical, but often there are custom fields or 3rd party solutions. Now with custom fields included there’s additional flexibility ensuring service reps have improved automation, prepossessing and context to quickly close cases.

 
 

 

More Information & Thank you!

For additional Q1 innovations relevant for SAP Enterprise Service Management, reference Kirsi Tarvainen’s blog on SAP Service Cloud and register for the upcoming Q1 Release Webinar, and to keep up as we release capabilities each month reference the What’s New in our help portal. Please also reference these valuable links for more on SAP’s CX Customer Service Solutions:

SAP learning ESM Course

SAP Service Cloud V2 Certification Course

SAP Sales & Service Cloud Innovation Spotlight Webinar

To learn more about our Q1/2026 innovations across SAP’s CX solutions check out the blog!

Use the SAP Enterprise Service Management managed tag when posting on SAP ESM topics!

Finally, an enormous thank you to this expanding community, for your active participation and collaboration; as we all benefit – SAP employees, partners and customers -  from sharing our experiences and information on SAP ESM.

entry.title: How to Display Position Code in Latest People Profile Header – SAP SuccessFactors EC Workaround
entry.content:
In the legacy People Profile of SAP SuccessFactors, the position code was displayed alongside the position name, making it easy for consultants and HR users to identify positions quickly.

However, in the latest People Profile (introduced after last upgrades), only the position name is displayed. The position code is no longer visible in the profile header, making it time-consuming for users to retrieve this information from the Position Org Chart.

To address this limitation, I explored a workaround solution that enables displaying the position code again in the latest People Profile header.

Legacy people profile – Earlier position code is available beside of the position name


Latest people profile- Only the position name is displayed, and the position code is no longer visible in the header.

Workaround Solution:
Step 1: Go to configure latest people profile > General settings


Step 2: Click on configure header fields

Step 3: Select first employee information type as Position

If Position Field Is Not Available in Dropdown - It needs to be synced to a UDF using HRIS Sync Mapping., For mapping Position field the UDF please follow the below steps.
3.1. Go to the Manage Business Configuration > Job Information > Position Field > Click on details

3.2. Add an HRIS Sync Mapping for the Position field. Map it to an unused standard element field ; for example, Customizable Field 7 can be used for this purpose. Once the mapping is done, click on Save.

3.3. Go to HRIS sync mapping check your added field weather is appearing or not.

3.4. Once HRIS Sync job will run the field data automatically available in the UDF. After that you can use this created Customizable field 7 “Position” in configure header fields.

Step 4: Save the general settings

Step 5: Navigate to the Employee Profile and Verify that the Position Code is now displayed along with the Position Name in the header

Conclusion

This workaround restores the visibility of the position code in the latest People Profile header by leveraging header field configuration and HRIS Sync Mapping. As a result, HR users and consultants can quickly identify positions without navigating to the Position Org Chart, improving efficiency and overall user experience.

entry.title: CSV Validator App Is Now Open Source
entry.content:
From Solution to Community Tool

A few weeks ago, I shared how CSVValidationApp helps solve data quality issues in SAP SuccessFactors Talent Intelligence Hub. The response surprised me. Many have built similar workarounds, spent hours cleaning data manually, or watched perfectly good imports fail because of stray HTML tags.

That's when I felt this shouldn't be "my tool." It should be our tool.

Today, CSV Validator App is officially open source and available on GitHub under the Apache 2.0 license: github.com/SAP/csv-validator

Why This Matters

I didn't build this tool because I love CSV files (I don't). I built it because I was tired of seeing good work derailed by preventable issues. An HR team prepares a skills library update, exports clean data, imports it into TIH—and suddenly descriptions are broken, special characters are corrupted, and they're troubleshooting instead of enabling their workforce.

It's frustrating. And it's common.

Making this open source isn't about recognition or downloads. It's about honesty: I don't have all the answers. You might be importing recruiting data with different quirks. You might support a region with specific character encoding needs. You might have found a validation rule I missed.

By opening the code, we can solve these problems together—transparently, collaboratively, and faster than any of us could alone.

What You're Getting

This is a complete SAP Fiori application you can run today:

Core Validation:

  • Detects encoding issues (UTF-8 compliance, BOM, null bytes)
  • Validates CSV structure (headers, delimiters, column consistency)
  • Identifies problematic content (HTML tags, entities, control characters, special characters)
  • Checks field lengths and data integrity

Auto-Correction:

  • Strips HTML tags while preserving content
  • Converts HTML entities to UTF-8 characters (  → space, ü → ü)
  • Properly escapes special characters
  • Generates a clean, download-ready corrected file

UI Features:

  • Drag-and-drop file upload
  • Interactive preview of your CSV
  • Filterable table showing issues by row and type
  • Side-by-side view of original vs. corrected content
  • One-click download of the corrected file

Privacy:

  • Everything runs locally in your browser
  • No uploads, no servers, no data leaves your machine

It's built with SAP UI5, follows SAP Fiori standards, and runs via npm. Five minutes from clone to validation.

Getting Started

git clone https://github.com/SAP/csv-validator.git
cd csv-validator
npm install
npm start

Open your browser to the app url, drop in a CSV file, and click Validate. That's it.
You can test by uploading your csv and press validate. Here is a sample screenshot of what you can expect:

 

Where We Go From Here

I have ideas for improvements—support for other SuccessFactors modules, batch processing, direct API integration. But I'm more interested in hearing yours.

If you use this tool and it helps, tell me what's missing. If it doesn't catch an issue it should, open a GitHub issue with an example. If you have a fix or enhancement, submit a pull request. If you just want to share your experience, leave a comment below.

This isn't a finished product. It's a starting point. I'm learning as much from your use cases as you might learn from the code.

An Honest Ask

If you work with SuccessFactors data imports—whether you're an administrator, consultant, or developer—try this tool with your real files. Not a sanitized demo file. Your actual data (with sensitive info removed, of course).

Then tell me:

  • Did it catch issues you would have missed?
  • Did it flag false positives that aren't actually problems?
  • What validation rule would make your life easier?

And if you've built something similar or have ideas for collaboration, let's talk. The goal isn't to centralize everything into this one tool—it's to raise the baseline for data quality across the community.

Resources:

  • GitHub Repository: github.com/SAP/csv-validator
  • Original Blog Post: Bridging Data Quality Gaps in TIH with CSVValidationApp
  • Issues & Feedback: github.com/SAP/csv-validator/issues
  • Contribution Guidelines: github.com/SAP/csv-validator/CONTRIBUTING.md

Data quality shouldn't be a bottleneck. Let's make it a strength—together.

entry.title: Assignment UUID for Deep Links in SAP SuccessFactors Latest People Profile
entry.content:
Assignment UUID for Deep Links in SAP SuccessFactors Latest People Profile

Introduction

With recent releases and the move to the Latest People Profile, many existing deep links in SAP SuccessFactors that navigate to a specific employee section or portlet in the legacy profile may no longer work as expected or may not work at all.

Previously, navigation to a specific employee was commonly done using the User ID (users-sys-id). With the latest People Profile, navigation is now based on Assignment UUID.

This is further detailed in the SAP documentation regarding deep links for profile categories and cards: Deep Links to Full Profile Categories and Cards 

Scenario

URL Format

Go to a specific category for a specific user

https://<app-server-domain>/sf/liveprofile?assignmentId=<assignment UUID>&categoryId=<category Id>

This example links to a user's Job Data category:

Example

https://www.example.com/sf/liveprofile?assignmentId=B2F70B823E3B4AD08091F6D1FBB74313&categoryId=jobData

If the <assignment UUID> is not specified in the URL, the deep link defaults to the signed-in user's profile rather than the intended employee's profile.

In this blog, we focus on how to navigate to a specific employee using the Assignment UUID, which is not readily available in SAP SuccessFactors and must be fetched using Integration Center for deep links.

Understanding User Identifiers in SAP SuccessFactors

SAP SuccessFactors uses multiple identifiers to represent a person and employment data in the system.

A full overview of the available Different User Identifiers in SAP SuccessFactors is available here: Different User Identifiers in SAP SuccessFactors

Assignment UUID

Field

Description

Assignment UUID

Assignment UUIDs are 32 characters and use all uppercase letters (for example: FDF7921CD3C242F59963B30E8895D04A). They are system-generated identifiers and are used in URLs and APIs without exposing personal employee data.

Can it be changed?

No

Can it be used for SSO?

No

Can it be used for integration?

Yes

Where is it displayed on People Profile?

NA

Making Assignment UUID Available for Dynamic Deep Links

The goal is to use the Assignment UUID as a dynamic token in deep links that navigate directly to an employee profile in SAP SuccessFactors.

To achieve this, the Assignment UUID must first be fetched and made available for reuse in templates or notifications. We can implement a custom solution using the Metadata Framework (MDF) and Integration Center.

Custom MDF to Store Assignment UUID

A custom MDF object is created to store the Assignment UUID. This allows the value to be reused across different use cases.

Fields include:

  • User ID
  • Assignment UUID

View of the Custom MDF object definition in Configure Object Definitions.

Integration Center Job to Populate MDF

An Integration Center job is used to fetch the Assignment UUID and populate the custom MDF object. This job should be configured to run daily to ensure that newly hired employees are included.

Integration Center field mapping, Assignment UUID mapped to the custom MDF object field.

After the Integration Center job execution, the MDF is successfully populated, as shown below. Each record now contains the corresponding Assignment UUID, ready for use in dynamic deep links.

"Manage Data" view of the custom MDF object showing records successfully populated with User IDs and their corresponding Assignment UUIDs.

Assignment UUID: 249B9F319A8B47FBA16B6537D529E7CA

Use Assignment UUID as a Dynamic Token in Deep Links

Once stored, the Assignment UUID can be used as a dynamic token in a URL in Document Generation via rule mapping, and when the notification is generated, the token is replaced dynamically.

Example deep link:

https://hcm-xxx.hr.cloud.sap/sf/liveprofile?assignmentId=[[AssignmentUUID]]&categoryId=jobData

Document Generation template displaying the alert message, with the clickable link and the [[AssignmentUUID]] token shown separately to illustrate dynamic value population.

Document Generation template editor displaying the [[AssignmentUUID]] token embedded within the URL link configuration.

Document Mapping Rule (Lookup Function)

Create a rule with a Document Mapping scenario to retrieve the Assignment UUID from MDF using a lookup function.

Business Rule using a Lookup() function to fetch the Assignment UUID from the custom MDF object..

Reference Rule in Document Template Mapping

Document Generation Mapping screen where the [[AssignmentUUID]] token is assigned to the Business Rule

Notifications with Assignment UUID Deep Links

The notification now generates a dynamic link with the Assignment UUID and directs the user to the corresponding employee profile in the Latest People Profile.

The email notification: 

Email notification displaying the clickable deep link populated with the dynamic Assignment UUID.

The Link:
https://hcm-xxx.hr.cloud.sap/sf/liveprofile?assignmentId=249B9F319A8B47FBA16B6537D529E7CA&categoryId=jobData

Successful Navigation to Employee Profile

Employee profile Job Data section displayed after clicking the deep link, confirming successful navigation using the dynamic Assignment UUID.

Summary 

This blog explained how to fetch the Assignment UUID and use it in URL links to navigate to a specific employee profile, category, or card in the Latest People Profile in SAP SuccessFactors. Since this identifier is not directly available in the system, a custom solution is implemented to retrieve and maintain it for dynamic use.

The solution is implemented as follows:

  • The Assignment UUID is retrieved using an Integration Center job and stored in a custom MDF object
  • A business rule with a lookup function dynamically fetches the value
  • The Assignment UUID is then used as a token and embedded into deep link URLs in the document generation template

Notes & Key Considerations

  • Job Frequency: Ensure your Integration Center job runs frequently enough to account for new hires.
  • URL Encoding: When building URLs, ensure there are no trailing spaces or characters that could break the string.
